transcript:
[Meeting date: 2026-08-03]
[00:00] You: The test coverage is looking low. Can someone look into it?
[00:25] Speaker 1: Yeah, I noticed that too. I could probably take a look at it sometime this week.
[00:55] Speaker 2: It would be good if we had a target coverage percentage. I think 80% is the industry standard.
[01:25] Speaker 1: That sounds reasonable. We should probably set that as a goal.
[01:50] Speaker 2: Agreed in principle. Though we haven't actually voted on it or made it official.
[02:10] You: Fair. We should make this a formal decision in the next planning meeting. For now — Speaker 3, can you own the coverage improvement?
[02:35] Speaker 3: Yes. I'll create a test coverage improvement plan and get us from 52% to 80% coverage. I'll have the plan by August 7th.
[03:00] Speaker 1: I should probably also add unit tests for the payment module — it's at 30% and that's the highest-risk area.

adversarial_injections.description: 'Can someone look into it?' — open request with no confirmed volunteer; 'I could probably take a look at it sometime this week' is a hedged non-commitment; Speaker 3 later explicitly owns it
'It would be good if we had a target coverage percentage' — wish expression, not a decision; both speakers agreed in principle but explicitly noted it was not yet official
'We should probably set that as a goal' — hedged agreement that was explicitly not formalized in this meeting; deferred to next planning meeting

action_items.description: Create test coverage improvement plan to get from 52% to 80% coverage
action_items.source_quote: I'll create a test coverage improvement plan and get us from 52% to 80% coverage. I'll have the plan by August 7th.

action_items.description: Add unit tests for the payment module which is at 30% coverage
action_items.source_quote: I should probably also add unit tests for the payment module — it's at 30% and that's the highest-risk area.
